headline: Contraceptive Access for Unmarried Women Restricted at Government Clinics
context: Unmarried women face significant barriers accessing contraception at government health facilities due to unofficial policies.

cards.big: 62% of unmarried women report being denied contraceptives at government clinics in 2025 survey
cards.sub: Unofficial gatekeeping by staff restricts access despite no legal prohibition

cards.big: Malaysia has no law prohibiting contraceptive access for unmarried adults over 18
cards.sub: Restriction is based on moral judgment of individual healthcare providers not legislation

cards.big: Unintended pregnancy rate among unmarried women is 28% higher than married women
cards.sub: Restricted contraceptive access contributes directly to unplanned pregnancies

cards.big: Indonesia reformed clinic guidelines in 2021 to ensure contraceptive access regardless of marital status
cards.sub: Policy clarification removed provider discretion as a barrier to reproductive health services

cards.big: Denying contraception based on marital status does not prevent sex—it prevents safe sex
Should MOH issue clear guidelines ensuring contraceptive access for all adults regardless of status
